name: rad-1password
description:
End-to-end 1Password CLI (op) coverage with a hardcoded-secrets scanner. Skills cover secret references (op://...), `op inject`/`op run`/`op read` for keeping secrets out of code and shell history, item CRUD with assignment statements and JSON templates, vault and user provisioning with granular permissions, SSH key generation and use, third-party CLI auth via `op plugin run`, and service-account / Connect-server patterns for CI/CD and headless servers. 1 router skill, 10 topical sub-skills, 4 slash commands.

**v1.1 adds** `scripts/scan-hardcoded-secrets.py` — pure-stdlib Python that scans source code for provider-prefixed tokens (OpenAI, Anthropic, GitHub PAT, Stripe, AWS, Slack, Google, Twilio, SendGrid, Mailgun, Supabase JWT), URL-embedded passwords (postgres/mongodb/mysql/redis/https/etc), and assignment-shaped credentials (`api_key = "..."`, `password: "..."`). For each finding, suggests a concrete `op://<vault>/<item>/<field>` reference shape suitable for replacement. Skips `.env*` local files, lockfiles, test fixtures, and obvious placeholders. Not a replacement for gitleaks/trufflehog — those have curated signature feeds; this validator is for in-editor use during development with explicit op:// migration suggestions. Cross-checked against op v2.34.0 --help.
